diagnosis: The level consists of a massive, uniform hill of solid blocks with a flat floor and no gaps, platforms, or elevation changes that require jumping. The player simply walks right for 28.8 seconds, takes one hit (likely from the three '1' enemies placed on the slope), and never reaches the exit. The playtester explicitly calls out the lack of terrain variance and over-reliance on 'hills on hills,' confirming the level fails to deliver platforming engagement.
lessons: A level composed of a single repeated macro-structure (e.g., one continuous slope) creates mechanical fatigue even if local geometry varies slightly.
Terrain variance requires distinct spatial contexts — gaps, platforms at multiple elevations, ceilings, and chokepoints — not just a continuous height gradient.
